root_text.an10.8:1.1: “Saddho ca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu hoti, no ca sīlavā;
root_text.an10.8:1.5: Yato ca kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saddho ca hoti sīlavā ca, evaṁ so tenaṅgena paripūro hoti.
root_text.an10.8:2.1: Saddho ca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu hoti sīlavā ca, no ca bahussuto …pe…
root_text.an10.8:2.4: parisāvacaro ca, no ca visārado parisāya dhammaṁ deseti …
root_text.an10.8:2.5: visārado ca parisāya dhammaṁ deseti, no ca vinayadharo …
root_text.an10.8:2.6: vinayadharo ca, no ca āraññiko pantasenāsano …
root_text.an10.8:2.7: āraññiko ca pantasenāsano, no ca catunnaṁ jhānānaṁ ābhicetasikānaṁ diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārānaṁ nikāmalābhī hoti akicchalābhī akasiralābhī …
root_text.an10.8:2.8: catunnañca jhānānaṁ ābhicetasikānaṁ diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārānaṁ nikāmalābhī hoti akicchalābhī akasiralābhī, no ca āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an10.8:2.11: ‘kintāhaṁ saddho ca assaṁ, sīlavā ca, bahussuto ca, dhammakathiko ca, parisāvacaro ca, visārado ca parisāya dhammaṁ deseyyaṁ, vinayadharo ca, āraññiko ca pantasenāsano, catunnañca jhānānaṁ ābhicetasikānaṁ diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārānaṁ nikāmalābhī assaṁ akicchalābhī akasiralābhī, āsavānañca khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja vihareyyan’ti.
root_text.an10.8:3.1: Yato ca kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saddho ca hoti, sīlavā ca, bahussuto ca, dhammakathiko ca, parisāvacaro ca, visārado ca parisāya dhammaṁ deseti, vinayadharo ca, āraññiko ca pantasenāsano, catunnañca jhānānaṁ ābhicetasikānaṁ diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārānaṁ nikāmalābhī hoti akicchalābhī akasiralābhī, āsavānañca khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati;
root_text.an10.8:3.3: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, dasahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu samantapāsādiko ca hoti sabbākāraparipūro cā”ti.

translation_text.an10.8:1.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant is faithful but not ethical.
translation_text.an10.8:1.2: So they’re incomplete in that respect,
translation_text.an10.8:1.4: ‘How can I become faithful and ethical?’
translation_text.an10.8:1.5: When the mendicant is faithful and ethical, they’re complete in that respect.
translation_text.an10.8:2.1: A mendicant is faithful and ethical, but not learned. …
translation_text.an10.8:2.4: they don’t teach Dhamma to the assembly with assurance …
translation_text.an10.8:2.5: they’re not an expert in the monastic law …
translation_text.an10.8:2.6: they don’t stay in the wilderness, in remote lodgings …
translation_text.an10.8:2.7: they don’t get the four absorptions—blissful meditations in this life that belong to the higher mind—when they want, without trouble or difficulty …
translation_text.an10.8:2.8: they don’t realize the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life, and live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an10.8:2.9: So they’re incomplete in that respect,
translation_text.an10.8:2.11: ‘How can I become faithful, ethical, and learned, a Dhamma speaker, one who frequents assemblies, one who teaches Dhamma to the assembly with assurance, an expert in the training, one who lives in the wilderness, in remote lodgings, one who gets the four absorptions when they want, and one who lives having realized the ending of defilements?’
translation_text.an10.8:3.1: When they’re faithful, ethical, and learned, a Dhamma speaker, one who frequents assemblies, one who teaches Dhamma to the assembly with assurance, an expert in the training, one who lives in the wilderness, in remote lodgings, one who gets the four absorptions when they want, and one who lives having realized the ending of defilements,
translation_text.an10.8:3.3: A mendicant who possesses these ten qualities is impressive all around, and is complete in every respect.”

variant_text.an10.8:1.1: ca → imasmiṁ vākye ayaṁ ca kāro natthi sya-all
